memory.deep_clean_desc: Deeply purge Kernel Caches (Standby List, Modified Page List). Suitable when the system is bloated due to file scanning or video playback.
memory.no_selection: Select a process to view details
memory.deep_clean_prompt:
Deep Clean will completely purge the System File Cache and Standby List to reclaim the maximum amount of RAM possible.

This action may slightly reduce disk read speeds in the first few minutes after cleaning.

Continue with Deep Clean?
memory.deep_clean_success: Awesome! Successfully reclaimed {{size}} of RAM from the system.
memory.deep_clean_done: Deep Clean completed (no significant additional RAM was reclaimed).
memory.deep_clean_desc_tooltip: Deeply purge all File Cache and Standby List to thoroughly reclaim RAM.
memory.admin_required_optimize: Please run the application as Administrator to optimize memory.
memory.admin_required_deep_clean: You need to run ResMonix as Administrator to use Deep Clean.

dashboard.title: System Overview
dashboard.subtitle: System resources overview
dashboard.select_drive: Select a drive to start analyzing

disk.name: Name
disk.scan_to_view: Scan a drive to view treemap
disk.select_drive_scan: Select a drive and click Scan to start
disk.access_denied: ⚠️ Access denied. Run as Administrator to view content.
disk.system_file_warning: 🔴 Important system file/folder. Should not be deleted.
disk.delete_confirm:
Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"?
Size: {{size}}

process_detail.confirm_kill: Are you sure you want to kill "{{name}}" (PID: {{pid}})?
process_detail.warning_system_process: ⚠️ WARNING: This is a system process, killing it may cause errors!
process_detail.kill_immediately: The process will be terminated immediately.
process_detail.system_process_warning: Critical system process. Terminating may cause errors or BSOD.
process_detail.summary_error_privileges: Could not read process details (PID {{pid}}). Administrator privileges might be required.
process_detail.error_open_failed: Could not open process (PID {{pid}}). Try running as Administrator.

kernel nonpaged pool.description: Operating system memory locked in RAM. Typically contains critical Kernel data, GPU drivers, and network drivers that cannot be swapped to disk.

kernel paged pool.description: Operating system memory that can be paged to disk (Pagefile) when RAM is low. Contains less critical system and application data.

hardware drivers / page tables.description: RAM occupied by Page Tables for memory management, File System Cache, and low-level hardware drivers not directly reported via Paged/NonPaged pool.

file system (ntfs).description: Cache storing directory structures (MFT) and NTFS drive metadata. Bloats when scanning files or copying large data.

ntfs file system.description: Cache storing directory structures (MFT) and NTFS drive metadata. Bloats when scanning files or copying large data.

file objects.description: Data structures representing files currently opened or recently closed by software.

memory manager section.description: Used to map files into memory (Memory Mapped Files) and manage shared memory.

thread objects.description: Data structures storing information about running processes and threads.

process objects.description: Data structures storing information about running processes and threads.

event tracing (etw).description: Buffer storing system logs and Windows diagnostic events.

registry config manager.description: Cache storing currently read/written Registry keys.

directx graphics kernel.description: Buffer used by the graphics card (GPU) to store textures, shaders, and interface with hardware.

video graphics kernel.description: Buffer used by the graphics card (GPU) to store textures, shaders, and interface with hardware.

ndis network driver.description: Network buffer used to receive, send, and process Internet packets (TCP/UDP).

tcp/ip protocol.description: Network buffer used to receive, send, and process Internet packets (TCP/UDP).

udp protocol.description: Network buffer used to receive, send, and process Internet packets (TCP/UDP).

network system.description: Network buffer used to receive, send, and process Internet packets (TCP/UDP).

ancillary function driver.description: Network buffer used to receive, send, and process Internet packets (TCP/UDP).

security tokens.description: Data verifying access rights for users and software.

security subsystem.description: Data verifying access rights for users and software.

system.description: The Windows operating system kernel, managing all hardware and software resources.

[system process].description: The Windows operating system kernel, managing all hardware and software resources.

csrss.exe.description: Core system process managing the windows console and threads. Killing causes BSOD.
csrss.exe.importance: Extremely Critical — killing causes Blue Screen

lsass.exe.description: Authenticates user logins, manages security policies and tokens.

smss.exe.description: Initializes Windows sessions, runs csrss.exe and winlogon.exe for each session.

winlogon.exe.description: Manages login/logout, handles Ctrl+Alt+Del, locks screen.

services.exe.description: Manages all Windows Services. Parent process of all svchost.exe.

memory compression.description: Compresses less frequently used memory pages to free physical RAM, reducing pagefile usage.
memory compression.importance: Important — helps optimize RAM, do not kill

registry.description: Manages Windows Registry hives in memory.

svchost.exe.description: Generic process hosting multiple Windows Services. Each instance runs different services (Windows Update, DHCP, Audio...).
svchost.exe.importance: Important — contains essential services, killing may cause system instability

explorer.exe.description: Desktop shell — manages Taskbar, Start Menu, File Explorer. Killing loses desktop but can be restarted.
explorer.exe.importance: Important but restartable — Task Manager > File > Run 'explorer.exe'

dwm.exe.description: Renders Windows graphical interface (composition, transparency, animations).

searchindexer.exe.description: Indexes files for fast searching in Start Menu and File Explorer.
searchindexer.exe.importance: Non-essential — can be killed if fast search is unneeded

searchhost.exe.description: Indexes files for fast searching in Start Menu and File Explorer.
searchhost.exe.importance: Non-essential — can be killed if fast search is unneeded

spoolsv.exe.description: Manages the print queue. Essential if using a printer.
spoolsv.exe.importance: Non-essential if not using a printer

runtimebroker.exe.description: Manages permissions for UWP/Microsoft Store apps. Each UWP app has 1 instance.

chrome.exe.description: Google Chrome web browser. Each tab and extension runs in a separate process (multi-process architecture).
chrome.exe.importance: Normal — can kill, loses open tabs

msedge.exe.description: Microsoft Edge web browser (Chromium-based). Multi-process like Chrome.

firefox.exe.description: Firefox web browser. Uses multi-process (Electrolysis) for tabs.

brave.exe.description: Brave web browser (Chromium-based) with built-in ad blocking.

code.exe.description: VS Code editor. Multi-process: main, renderer, extension host, terminal.
code.exe.importance: Normal — can kill, save files first

devenv.exe.description: Visual Studio Integrated Development Environment. Uses heavy RAM for IntelliSense and building.
devenv.exe.importance: Normal — can kill, save project first

node.exe.description: JavaScript runtime. Typically a dev server, build tool, or backend service.
node.exe.importance: Normal — killing stops running dev server/script

python.exe.description: Runs Python scripts/apps. Could be a script, web server, ML training...

pythonw.exe.description: Runs Python scripts/apps. Could be a script, web server, ML training...

python3.exe.description: Runs Python scripts/apps. Could be a script, web server, ML training...

java.exe.description: Runs Java applications (IntelliJ IDEA, Minecraft, enterprise apps...).
java.exe.importance: Normal — killing stops running Java app

javaw.exe.description: Runs Java applications (IntelliJ IDEA, Minecraft, enterprise apps...).
javaw.exe.importance: Normal — killing stops running Java app

cargo.exe.description: Build tool and package manager for Rust. Typically building/compiling a project.

git.exe.description: Source code version control system. Currently performing a git operation.
git.exe.importance: Normal — killing may corrupt index if writing

vmmem.description: Memory representation of WSL2/Hyper-V VM. Size = RAM used by VM. Reduce by stopping WSL: 'wsl --shutdown'.
vmmem.importance: Important if using WSL — 'wsl --shutdown' to free RAM

vmmemwsl.description: Memory representation of WSL2/Hyper-V VM. Size = RAM used by VM. Reduce by stopping WSL: 'wsl --shutdown'.
vmmemwsl.importance: Important if using WSL — 'wsl --shutdown' to free RAM

vmwp.exe.description: Worker process for each running Hyper-V virtual machine.

wslhost.exe.description: Host process for WSL. Manages Linux distributions and Windows inter-op.
wslhost.exe.importance: Required for WSL — 'wsl --shutdown' to properly exit

wsl.exe.description: Host process for WSL. Manages Linux distributions and Windows inter-op.
wsl.exe.importance: Required for WSL — 'wsl --shutdown' to properly exit

wslservice.exe.description: Host process for WSL. Manages Linux distributions and Windows inter-op.
wslservice.exe.importance: Required for WSL — 'wsl --shutdown' to properly exit

com.docker.backend.exe.description: Docker container runtime. Manages and runs containers.
com.docker.backend.exe.importance: Required if using containers — exit via Docker Desktop

dockerd.exe.description: Docker container runtime. Manages and runs containers.
dockerd.exe.importance: Required if using containers — exit via Docker Desktop

docker.exe.description: Docker container runtime. Manages and runs containers.
docker.exe.importance: Required if using containers — exit via Docker Desktop

teams.exe.description: Microsoft Teams meeting and chat app.
teams.exe.importance: Normal — can kill when not in a meeting

ms-teams.exe.description: Microsoft Teams meeting and chat app.
ms-teams.exe.importance: Normal — can kill when not in a meeting

steam.exe.description: Steam gaming platform. steamwebhelper.exe is the integrated browser.
steam.exe.importance: Normal — can kill when not gaming

steamwebhelper.exe.description: Steam gaming platform. steamwebhelper.exe is the integrated browser.
steamwebhelper.exe.importance: Normal — can kill when not gaming

msmpeng.exe.description: Windows Defender virus/malware scanning engine. May use high CPU during scans.

securityhealthservice.exe.description: Monitors the overall security health of Windows.

kernel_pool_tag.description: A low-level Pool Tag of the Kernel or Hardware Driver allocating direct RAM memory.

temp_files.title: 🗑️ Temporary Files
temp_files.description: Temporary files that are no longer needed. Safe to delete.

cache.title: 🌐 Browser & App Cache
cache.description: Cache from browsers and applications. Deleting will not affect data.

logs.title: 📝 Log Files
logs.description: Log files from applications and the system. Safe to delete.

crash_dumps.title: 💥 Crash Dumps
crash_dumps.description: Crash dump files from crashed applications. Safe to delete if debugging is not needed.

development.title: 📦 Node Modules
development.description: Found {{count}} node_modules folders. Can be deleted and run 'npm install' to reinstall.

recycle_bin.title: ♻️ Recycle Bin
recycle_bin.description: Data in the Recycle Bin. Permanently delete to free up space.

python_cache.title: 🐍 Python Cache
python_cache.description: Python bytecode cache (__pycache__). Safe to delete, Python will regenerate them.

windows_component_store.description: Stores DLL versions and drivers for Windows Update. Cannot be deleted manually.
windows_component_store.importance: Critical — system will fail if deleted

wsl_docker_virtual_disk.description: Virtual disk containing all Linux/Docker containers data. Use 'docker system prune' + 'Optimize-VHD' to shrink.
wsl_docker_virtual_disk.importance: Important — contains containers data, do not delete manually

hibernation_file.description: Hibernation file, size equals RAM. Disable hibernation using 'powercfg -h off' to delete.
hibernation_file.importance: Can be disabled if hibernation is not used

virtual_memory_page_file.description: Virtual memory page file, used by Windows when RAM is full. Do not delete directly.
virtual_memory_page_file.importance: Critical — system requires it for stability

node_js_dependencies.description: JavaScript dependencies of the project. Can be deleted and run 'npm install' or 'bun install' to reinstall.

git_repository_data.description: Contains full version control history. Deleting it will lose all commit history.
git_repository_data.importance: Important — contains code history, push to remote before deleting

windows_update_cache.description: Cache of downloaded Windows updates. Safe to delete, Windows will download again when needed.
windows_update_cache.importance: Safe to delete — use Disk Cleanup to delete properly
